# pdf2htmlEX AI Guide

This repository is a patched `pdf2htmlEX` codebase. It converts PDFs to HTML by
using Poppler for PDF parsing/render callbacks, FontForge for font conversion,
and C++ code under `pdf2htmlEX/src` to emit HTML, CSS, fonts, and page
background assets. Python is used for tests and helpers, not converter runtime.

Start here:

- Human docs index: `docs/README.md`
- Full AI guide: `llms-full.txt`
- User quick start: `docs/user/quick-start.md`
- Wiki-derived source notes: `docs/reference/wiki-sources.md`
- Architecture: `docs/architecture/overview.md`
- Design solutions: `docs/architecture/design-solutions.md`
- Rendering pipeline: `docs/architecture/rendering-pipeline.md`
- Build: `docs/build/build-from-source.md`
- Tests: `docs/development/testing.md`
- Internals: `docs/internals/`

Key source areas:

- `pdf2htmlEX/src/pdf2htmlEX.cc`: CLI, options, PDF open, renderer startup
- `pdf2htmlEX/src/HTMLRenderer/`: main conversion callbacks
- `pdf2htmlEX/src/BackgroundRenderer/`: PNG/JPEG/SVG page backgrounds
- `pdf2htmlEX/src/HTMLTextPage.*`, `HTMLTextLine.*`: HTML text output
- `pdf2htmlEX/src/StateManager.h`: generated CSS classes
- `pdf2htmlEX/src/util/ffw.*`: FontForge wrapper
- `pdf2htmlEX/share/manifest`: final HTML assembly template
- `pdf2htmlEX/test/`: Python and browser tests
- `buildScripts/`: Poppler/FontForge/converter build automation

Wiki material has been localized into `docs/user/`,
`docs/build/prebuilt-packages.md`, and `docs/reference/wiki-sources.md`.
Treat wiki-derived old-information pages as historical unless corroborated by
current files.

Verified during documentation:

- Existing binary `pdf2htmlEX/build/pdf2htmlEX -v` ran.
- `python3 pdf2htmlEX/test/test_output.py` passed 23 tests.
- `ctest --test-dir pdf2htmlEX/build -R test_basic --output-on-failure` passed.
- Rebuilding with local CMake 4.3.0 failed because CMake rejects the project's
  old `cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 2.6.0)`.

For code changes, read `llms-full.txt`, inspect the relevant internals doc, run
targeted tests, and update docs when behavior changes.
